dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7719 IH: Securing Our Streets Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Wied (for himself and Mr. Tiffany) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To require the Attorney General to administer grants to discourage repeat offenders.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Securing Our Streets Act of 2026 or the SOS Act of 2026.
Title I of the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 (34 U.S.C. 10101 et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: Beginning not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this part, the Attorney General shall carry out a program under which the Attorney General makes grants to States to.
A State seeking a grant under this part shall submit to the Attorney General an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Attorney General may require.
To be eligible to receive a grant under this part, a State shall have in effect a law or policy that requires—

paragraph.text: repeat offenders to serve at least 85 percent of the sentenced imposed;
pretrial detention of individuals charged with a crime of violence who pose a clear threat to public safety; and
life imprisonment for individuals convicted of 3 distinct offenses, arising out of different conduct occurring at different times, that constitute crimes of violence.

subsection.text: A State that receives a grant under this part shall use such grant to—

paragraph.text: expand operational capacity to incarcerate offenders who have committed crimes of violence; and

section.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this part $10,000,000,000 for fiscal years 2027 through 2031.
